text: Last class I wrote a clear follow-up email after the meeting.
Today I want to reply to a customer and offer a clear solution.

Acknowledge
Apologize
Inconvenience
Resolve
Replacement

Delivery
We apologize for the late delivery.
We are sorry for the inconvenience.
We will send a replacement part this week.
We can offer a replacement, free of charge.
Hi Karen, I wrote the reply to Mr. Costa's complaint. I start by saying we acknowledge his email. Can you check it?
Good start. Do you apologize for the problem? The customer needs to feel we are sorry.
Yes. I wrote: We apologize for the late delivery, and we are sorry for the inconvenience.
Perfect. Now the solution. Remember, no full refund -- we offer a replacement. What did you write?
I wrote: We will send a replacement part by express delivery, free of charge, and we will resolve this by Friday.
That is clear and professional. You acknowledged, you apologized, and you offered a solution. Add me in CC and send it. Great work, Percival.
Thank you for your email. We acknowledge your complaint.
We will send a replacement part, free of charge.
We will resolve this problem by Friday.

Hello, this is a message for the customer service team at EGEA Saneamento. My name is Mr. Costa, and I am calling about a problem with my recent order. The delivery arrived two days late, and one of the parts was damaged. This is a real inconvenience for my company, because we could not start the work on time. I would like you to resolve this quickly. I am not asking for a full refund, but I do need a replacement part as soon as possible. Please acknowledge my complaint by email today, and tell me when the new part will arrive. Thank you, and I hope to hear from you soon.

Hi Percival, this is Karen. I read the complaint from Mr. Costa, and I would like you to write the reply. Please keep it calm and professional. First, acknowledge his email, so he knows we received it. Then apologize clearly: we are sorry for the late delivery and the damaged part. Do not offer a full refund, but offer a solution: we will send a replacement part by express delivery, free of charge. Tell him we will resolve the problem this week, and that we apologize for the inconvenience. End politely, and send me the draft before it goes out. Thank you so much.

First, Percival reads the customer complaint about the late delivery and the damaged part. Then, he acknowledges the email, so the customer knows it arrived. After that, he apologizes for the inconvenience. Later, he offers a solution: a replacement part by express delivery. Finally, he tells the customer when the problem will be resolved, and closes the email politely.
